narrative:
The market sits firmly in a business as usual regime, with a stress score of 11 out of 100 signalling an unusually calm environment. Broad equity momentum is modestly positive, with SPY up 1.6% over the past five days at a z-score well within normal range, and there are no structural pressures pulling the regime toward deterioration.

VIX at 15.7 sits at only the 26th percentile of its ten-year history, and the term structure is in normal contango, meaning options markets are pricing in no near-term disruption. Credit is strongly corroborating that picture: high yield spreads at 273 basis points sit at just the 8th percentile of their five-year range, and investment grade at 74 basis points carries a z-score of negative 1.22, both indicating spreads are historically tight and lenders are pricing risk with notable confidence.

XLE is the one sector standing out to the downside, off 4.8% over five days, while FXI has lost 3.4%, suggesting some specific softness in energy and Chinese equities worth monitoring. Against that, XLK has gained 5.5% and EEM is up 4.8%, meaning the weakness in those pockets is being more than offset by strength in technology and broader emerging market exposure elsewhere.
